title: 11 Proven Herbal Tinctures for Flu Symptoms

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for flu because they have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties that help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms, fight off the virus, and speed up recovery.
Cichoric acid is good for flu because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the virus, making it easier for the body to recover from illness.
Quercetin is good for flu because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, including fever and body aches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is made from the Echinacea purpurea plant, also known as coneflower. This plant has been used for many years to help prevent and treat the flu. It contains compounds that help boost the immune system, which is the body's defense against illness. When we get the flu, our immune system fights the virus, but it can get tired quickly. Echinacea purpurea tincture may help keep the immune system strong by reducing inflammation and fighting off the virus. It may also help reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, such as fever, sore throat, and body aches. Some people take Echinacea purpurea tincture at the first sign of the flu to help their immune system fight off the virus, and others take it as a preventative measure to boost their immune system and reduce the risk of getting the flu.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tinctures that help with flu, you will need dried Astragalus roots and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, prepare a clean glass jar by washing it with soap and water, and then dry it with a clean towel. Fill the jar about one-quarter full with the dried Astragalus roots, and then add the solvent, making sure that the roots are completely covered. The general ratio is one part dried roots to two parts solvent, but you can adjust this according to your needs and preferences. Next, seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing it to steep for two to four weeks. Shake the jar gently every day or two to help the solvent extract the active compounds from the roots. After the steeping period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Astragalus membranaceus tincture is now ready to use as a remedy for flu, taken as directed by a healthcare professional or according to the label instructions.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for flu

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for flu because they help boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and fight off viral infections, making them a natural remedy to alleviate flu symptoms.
Iridoid glycosides are good for flu because they have strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce fever and fight off viral infections in the body.
Iridoids help fight the flu by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation in the body, which makes it easier for the body to recover from the illness.
Cichoric acid is good for flu because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fever in the body, making it easier to recover from the illness.

Quercetin in Astragalus membranaceos is good for flu because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as fever and body aches, while also boosting the immune system to fight off the virus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia tincture is a liquid extract made from the roots of the Echinacea angustifolia plant. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to help prevent and treat colds and flu. The tincture contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties and can help boost the immune system. When taken as a supplement, it may help reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, such as fever, sore throat, and fatigue. The tincture may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of coughs and congestion. Some people find that taking Echinacea angustifolia tincture at the first sign of illness can help to shorten the duration of their symptoms and get them back to feeling better faster. Additionally, the tincture may help to reduce the risk of complications from flu, such as pneumonia. However, it is essential to note that the tincture is not a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in conjunction with other treatments as recommended by a healthcare professional.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tincture that helps with flu, you will need dried Astragalus roots and a few simple equipment. First, you need to prepare the Astragalus roots by cleaning and drying them, then chop them into small pieces. Next, you will need a glass jar with a lid, a measuring cup, and a solvent such as vodka or glycerin. Fill the jar with the chopped Astragalus roots, making sure they are covered by the solvent, and then add enough solvent to fill the jar. The general ratio is one part of herbs to two parts of solvent. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for about two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs steep and release their properties. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is your Astragalus membranaceus tincture, which can be taken as is or diluted with water. It is recommended to take 20-30 drops of the tincture three times a day to help boost your immune system and fight off flu symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea angustifolia herbal tinctures for flu

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for flu because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and relieve congestion.
Shogaol is good for flu because it has strong antiviral properties that can help stop the flu virus from spreading and causing more harm in the body.
Zingerone is good for flu because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity of flu symptoms and boost the immune system to fight the infection.
Gingerols are good for flu because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and alleviate congestion in the body, making it easier to recover from the illness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help with flu symptoms. The tincture, made from concentrated ginger extract, contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory properties. This can help reduce fever and ease body aches. Ginger's natural antiviral properties may also help fight the influenza virus itself. Furthermore, ginger has been traditionally used to soothe a sore throat and calm nausea, two common flu complaints. Additionally, ginger's warm and spicy flavor can help stimulate digestion and relieve congestion, making it easier to breathe.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tincture, start by gathering dried Astragalus root or purchasing it from a health food store. Fill a clean glass jar about one-quarter full with the dried root. Next, pour a high-proof spirit such as vodka or brandy over the root, making sure the plant is completely covered. The general ratio for this tincture is 1 part dried root to 2 parts spirit. For example, if you're using 1 cup of dried root, use 2 cups of vodka. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for at least 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herbs infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. This tincture can be taken as needed when you feel the flu coming on, by mixing a few drops with water or another liquid. It's essential to note that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have underlying health conditions or are pregnant/breastfeeding.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for flu

Isorhapontigenin is good for flu because it has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help reduce symptoms such as fever, cough, and fatigue, and also boost the immune system to fight off the virus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as black elderberry, is a popular herbal remedy for flu. The tincture is made from the berries of the plant, which contain powerful antioxidants and flavonoids that help fight off the flu virus. When taken at the first sign of symptoms, the Sambucus nigra tincture can help reduce the severity and duration of the flu. It works by blocking the flu virus's ability to attach to and infect cells in the body, which can help prevent the virus from spreading and causing further damage. Additionally, the tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, headache, and body aches, making it easier to recover from the flu. By supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation, the Sambucus nigra tincture can help the body fight off the flu and recover faster.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tincture, start by gathering fresh or dried Astragalus roots. If using dried roots, you can skip to the next step. If using fresh roots, dry them in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for 2-3 hours. Once the roots are dry, combine them with 80-100% vodka or a similar high-proof spirit in a clean glass jar. A general ratio is 1 part Astragalus roots to 2 parts vodka. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day for 2-6 weeks to help the herbs infuse into the liquid. After the infusion time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is your Astragalus membranaceus tincture, which can be taken 2-3 times a day to help boost the immune system and fight off the flu.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for flu because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as fever and sore throat, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines in the body.

Quercetin is good for flu because it has powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as fever, body aches, and congestion, while also boosting the immune system to fight off the infection.
Eucalyptol is good for flu because it has natural decongestant properties that help to relieve congestion and coughs by breaking down mucus and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Cineole is good for flu because it has antimicrobial properties that help fight off the virus, reduce inflammation in the body, and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and recover from the illness.
Tannins are good for flu because they have antiviral properties that help fight the influenza virus and reduce inflammation in the body, making it easier to recover from the illness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is a natural remedy that can help with the flu. It contains compounds like eucalyptol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling in the nose and sinuses, making it easier to breathe when you're congested. The tincture also has a decongestant effect, which helps loosen mucus and make it easier to cough up. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus has antiviral properties, which may help fight off the flu virus. When taken internally, the tincture can also help reduce fever and alleviate body aches. Some people use it as a complementary treatment to help manage flu symptoms, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you're taking other medications or have underlying health conditions.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tincture that helps with flu, start by gathering dried Astragalus roots and leaves. You can purchase these from a health food store or online. Next, fill a clean glass jar with 1 part dried Astragalus and 3 parts 80-100 proof vodka or glycerin. The vodka is used to extract the herbs' active compounds, while glycerin is a gentler option for those who prefer it. Make sure the herbs are completely covered by the liquid, then seal the jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process is called maceration and it allows the herbs to release their healing properties into the liquid. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. The Astragalus tincture can be taken as needed to help boost the immune system and prevent or alleviate flu symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea pallida tincture is a natural remedy that can help your body fight the flu. It comes from a plant called Echinacea, which is also known as the coneflower. This plant has been used for a long time to boost the immune system and prevent illnesses like the flu. The tincture is made by soaking the Echinacea plant in a liquid, usually alcohol or glycerin. When you take the tincture, the active ingredients in the Echinacea plant, such as alkylamides and glycosides, go into your body and start working. These ingredients can help reduce inflammation, fight off germs, and make your body stronger. First, fill the jar about a quarter full with the dried Astragalus roots, making sure they are completely covered by the jar's rim. Then, pour vodka or glycerin over the roots until the jar is nearly full. intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a good alternative for flu treatment because they are natural and can provide relief without the harsh side effects of medication. Two herbs commonly used in flu treatment are Echinacea purpurea and Echinacea angustifolia. Echinacea purpurea is known for its ability to boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms. It contains compounds called alkylamides and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate respiratory issues. Echinacea angustifolia, on the other hand, is rich in polysaccharides that stimulate the production of white blood cells, helping the body fight off the flu virus. By using herbal tinctures containing these herbs, you can potentially reduce the risk of complications and shorten the recovery time. This is important because the flu can lead to serious health issues, such as pneumonia, bronchitis, and even hospitalization. By taking preventative measures and using herbal remedies, you can improve your quality of life and reduce the risk of flu-related problems. Regular use of herbal tinctures containing Echinacea purpurea and Echinacea angustifolia may also help to boost your immune system, making you less susceptible to future illnesses.
supplementary_best: To remedy the flu, a combination of Echinacea purpurea, Echinacea angustifolia, and Andrographis paniculata tinctures is recommended. Echinacea purpurea helps boost the immune system and fight off infections, while Echinacea angustifolia supports the body's natural defense mechanisms. Andrographis paniculata has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and alleviate body aches.  The addition of Zingiber officinale tincture aids in alleviating nausea and congestion, which are common symptoms of the flu. Sambucus nigra tincture helps relieve coughs and sore throats. A small amount of Eucalyptus globulus tincture can be added to ease congestion and make breathing easier.  Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is added for its soothing properties, which can calm inflammation and irritation in the throat. This combination of herbs is effective because it addresses multiple aspects of the flu, such as immune system support, anti-inflammatory properties, and relief of common symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have the flu, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that can make your symptoms worse. One such herb is 'Valeriana officinalis' (Valerian root), as it can slow down your breathing and worsen respiratory problems. Another herb to avoid is 'Aconitum napellus' (Aconite), which can cause nausea and vomiting, making flu symptoms even more unbearable. 'Hydrastis canadensis' (Goldenseal) should also be avoided, as it can interact with medications and worsen flu symptoms. 'Cinchona officinalis' (Cinchona) contains quinine, which can be toxic in large amounts and interact with medications. 'Rauwolfia serpentina' (Indian snakeroot) can also be problematic, as it can lower blood pressure and interact with medications. Lastly, 'Digitalis purpurea' (Foxglove) is toxic and can cause heart problems, making it a serious risk for people with the flu.
supplementary_other_remedies: When it comes to fighting the flu, there are several natural remedies that can help alleviate symptoms. One such remedy is steam inhalation. By inhaling steam from a bowl of hot water or a steam humidifier, the warm mist helps loosen and clear mucus from the nose and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful when dealing with congestion and sinus pressure. Another natural remedy is honey, which has antibacterial properties that can help soothe a sore throat and calm a cough. When taken warm, honey can also help loosen mucus and make it easier to cough up. Additionally, taking a warm bath or shower can help relax the body and reduce fever, which is often a symptom of the flu. This can be especially helpful when dealing with body aches and fatigue. Apple cider vinegar is also a natural remedy that can help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the flu. It is believed to have antiviral properties that can help fight off the virus, and it can also help replenish fluids and electrolytes in the body. Drinking apple cider vinegar mixed with water throughout the day can help alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
supplementary_related_ailments: When people have the flu, they often experience other ailments that can be painful and uncomfortable. One common issue is a sore throat, which can be relieved with medicinal herbs like slippery elm and licorice root. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the throat and reduce swelling.  Another common problem is a cough, which can be calmed with herbs like thyme and marshmallow root. Thyme is an expectorant, meaning it helps bring up mucus and phlegm from the lungs, while marshmallow root is a demulcent that coats and soothes the respiratory tract.  People with the flu may also experience fever, which can be reduced with herbs like yarrow and elderflower. Yarrow contains compounds that help lower body temperature, while elderflower has antiviral properties that help combat the underlying infection.  Additionally, the flu can cause body aches and pains, which can be eased with herbs like willow bark and ginger. Willow bark contains salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, while ginger has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and swelling.  Finally, some people with the flu may experience digestive issues like nausea and vomiting, which can be alleviated with herbs like peppermint and chamomile. Peppermint has a calming effect on the stomach, while chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system.  These medicinal herbs have been used for centuries to help alleviate the symptoms of the flu and other related ailments. They work by targeting specific areas of the body and providing relief from symptoms like pain, inflammation, and digestive issues.
